name: Torremolinos Passes Trail
name_original: Puertos de Torremolinos – circular por Los Pinillos y Cañada del Lobo
description: Hike the Ports of Torremolinos circular route for moderate challenge, panoramic Costa del Sol views, and a unique wolf sculpture.
region_intro: Hiking around Torremolinos offers diverse landscapes, from coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea to mountainous trails in the Sierra de Mijas. The region features pine forests, scrubland, and elevated viewpoints providing panoramic vistas. Trails often ascend through varied terrain, including areas with olive groves and cork oak forests. This geographical mix provides a range of hiking experiences for different ability levels. Best hiking trails around Torremolinos The most popular hiking route is Torremolinos Passes Trail, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through the local hills. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico Palomas – circular from Torremolinos via Puerto Canuto, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This circular route offers views of the surrounding mountainous terrain and can be completed in about 3 hours 1 minute. Local hikers also love the Sendero de los muertos R-1 variante Calamorro — circular desde Arroyo de la Miel, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Hiking around Torremolinos is defined by coastal paths, pine forests, and mountainous ascents in the Sierra de Mijas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging climbs. The routes in Torremolinos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 8000 hikers have used komoot to explore Torremolinos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Torremolinos?
A: There are over 130 hiking trails around Torremolinos, offering a wide variety of experiences. These include 67 easy routes, 52 moderate routes, and 11 more challenging trails, ensuring options for all fitness levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Torremolinos?
A: Hiking in Torremolinos offers a diverse mix of landscapes. You can explore coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea, mountainous trails in the Sierra de Mijas, and areas with pine forests, scrubland, olive groves, and cork oak forests. Many routes provide panoramic vistas of the coastline and, on clear days, even the Atlas Mountains.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly walks in Torremolinos?
A: Yes, Torremolinos has 67 easy routes suitable for families. The Río Higuerón Trail, for example, is a delightful and easily accessible hike following the river's course, offering shaded paths and a peaceful ambiance. The Molino de Inca Botanical Garden also provides a tranquil setting for a leisurely stroll.

Q: What are some good options for circular hikes near Torremolinos?
A: For circular routes, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995545062/pico-palomas-circular-from-torremolinos-via-puerto-canuto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Palomas – circular from Torremolinos via Puerto Canuto&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.5 km path offering views of the mountainous terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304250392/sendero-de-los-muertos-r-1-variante-calamorro-circular-desde-arroyo-de-la-Miel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de los muertos R-1 variante Calamorro — circular desde Arroyo de la Miel&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.0 km trail through varied landscapes.

Q: Where can I find trails with great viewpoints in the Torremolinos area?
A: Many trails in the Sierra de Mijas offer stunning viewpoints. The Cañada del Lobo (Wolf's Glen) trail is known for one of the finest views of the Costa del Sol. Peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2697276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palomas Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/633802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Calamorro&lt;/a&gt; also provide breathtaking panoramic vistas of the coastline and surrounding mountains.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Torremolinos?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Torremolinos, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from coastal walks to challenging mountain ascents, and the stunning panoramic views available from elevated points.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, there are 11 difficult routes for experienced trekkers. The Gran Senda de Málaga, for instance, includes stage 34 which crosses Torremolinos and links Benalmádena and Alhaurín de la Torre, presenting a significant challenge with an overall gradient of 1060 meters.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several natural attractions. The Molino de Inca Botanical Garden is a tranquil oasis with diverse plant species. For unique geological formations, consider a day trip to El Torcal de Antequera. You can also find impressive peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4019360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendoza Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2716495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de Medialuna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles accessible from hiking routes?
A: While hiking, you can find historical elements in the region. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7262649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Alcazaba of Málaga and the Roman Theatre&lt;/a&gt; are significant historical sites nearby. Additionally, the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/90115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colomares Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a notable man-made monument in the vicinity.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Torremolinos?
A: Torremolinos offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for exploring both coastal and mountain trails. Even in winter, the climate is often mild enough for enjoyable walks, while summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, especially for more exposed routes.

Q: Are there any coastal walks in Torremolinos?
A: Yes, Torremolinos is well-known for its coastal walks. The Senda Litoral (Coastal Walk) stretches across the Costa del Sol, with Stage 6 passing along the Torremolinos coastline. This medium-difficulty hike offers views of the Mediterranean and various beaches like Los Álamos, Playamar, El Bajondillo, La Carihuela, and El Saltillo. A popular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26765630" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torremolinos Beach Promenade – Puerto Marina, Benalmádena loop from Los Álamos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find trails that go through pine forests or natural parks?
A: Absolutely. The Los Pinares area on the outskirts of Torremolinos is a true green lung for the town, offering extensive pine forests and scrubland ideal for walking. Additionally, the Montes de Málaga Natural Park, a short drive away, features a network of trails winding through ancient olive groves, cork oak forests, and aromatic scrubland.
tips: One of the ports of the path that runs through the pass and that already allows you to have beautiful views towards the coast of Torremolinos.
The tower is a bit hidden and cannot be seen from the path. But it offers a great view of the mountains beyond the coast.
Great views of the snowy Sierra Nevada (in April) and the Rif Mountains in Morocco.

Be careful between January and April, as there may be processionary caterpillars in the pines. They are very dangerous, especially for dogs, because they can cause severe and even fatal burns. They are also dangerous to human skin, so contact should be avoided.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Ports of Torremolinos circular hike?
faqs.answer: This moderate hike usually takes about 3 hours and 29 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace and how long you spend enjoying the viewpoints.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Ports of Torremolinos - circular by Los Pinillos y Cañada del Lobo trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate. It involves an elevation gain of approximately 479 meters (around 1,575 feet) and requires good fitness and sure-footedness, especially on some sections.

faqs.question: Where is the starting point for the Ports of Torremolinos - circular by Los Pinillos y Cañada del Lobo trail, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The starting point for this circular route is conveniently located next to a parking lot, making it easily accessible for those arriving by car.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, primarily consisting of hiking paths, footpaths, and general paths. Most of the surfaces are unpaved and natural, winding through a dense pine forest with Mediterranean vegetation. There is a small section on streets.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints and attractions along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several stunning spots. You'll find panoramic views at the Wind Pass Viewpoint and the Cañada del Lobo Viewpoint, which can offer views of the Sierra Nevada and even the Rif Mountains on clear days. Don't miss the Mirador Tower and the iconic howling wolf sculpture at the 'Wolf Viewpoint'.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike the Ports of Torremolinos - circular by Los Pinillos y Cañada del Lobo trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring (April, for potential Sierra Nevada snow views) and autumn are particularly pleasant. Be aware that between January and April, processionary caterpillars can be present in the pine trees, especially around Puerto del Blanquillo.

faqs.question: Are there any specific natural features or wildlife to look out for?
faqs.answer: The trail winds through a beautiful pine forest, also featuring holm oaks, cork trees, wild olive trees, and palmettos. While not a primary wildlife spotting trail, you'll experience typical Mediterranean flora. As an insider tip, be mindful of processionary caterpillars in the pine trees from January to April.

faqs.question: Is the Ports of Torremolinos - circular by Los Pinillos y Cañada del Lobo trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and for the safety of other hikers, especially given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike this trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While the trail is close to Torremolinos, specific facilities like cafes or restrooms directly at the trailhead are not mentioned. It's advisable to bring your own water and snacks, and plan for facilities in Torremolinos town center before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Puertos de Torremolinos trail (86% of the route covered) and smaller portions of the Benalmádena - Alhaurín de la Torre and Sendero Puerto de las Ovejas - Calamorro trails.
